title: Charlie Catches a Leprechaun

jokes.text: FRANK: I slept 26 hours last night.
jokes.setup: Dennis says Frank will fall asleep during his presentation
jokes.punchline: Frank claims he slept 26 hours in one night, which is impossible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - confidently stating something impossible to deflect criticism. Economical and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I slept 26 hours last night.

jokes.text: Frank interrupting Dennis repeatedly about easels and projectors
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to start a serious business presentation
jokes.punchline: Frank constantly interrupting with unhelpful suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Frank's inability to let Dennis speak

jokes.text: CHARLIE: he played the flute for some kids, and then he lured them into a cave somewhere, and he diddled them.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what St. Patrick actually did
jokes.punchline: Charlie confuses St. Patrick with the Pied Piper and adds molestation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie brain - confidently mixing up folklore while adding inappropriate elements. Character-specific confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: he played the flute for some kids, and then he lured them into a cave somewhere, and he diddled them.

jokes.text: FRANK: He drove the kids out of Ireland. And then he molested 'em.
jokes.setup: Frank corrects Charlie's St. Patrick confusion
jokes.punchline: He gets the snakes/kids wrong but keeps the molestation part
jokes.explanation: Frank doubles down on the inappropriate element while still getting the facts wrong. Good escalation of Charlie's error.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: He drove the kids out of Ireland. And then he molested 'em.

jokes.text: FRANK: I feel like, in the olden days of the Catholic Church, pedophiles were regarded as heroes.
jokes.setup: Dennis points out pedophiles aren't heroes
jokes.punchline: Frank suggests they were heroes in old Catholic Church
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark but well-crafted subversion. Frank's warped historical perspective creates uncomfortable truth.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I feel like, in the olden days of the Catholic Church, pedophiles were regarded as heroes.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: I'm, like, this close.
jokes.setup: Gang dismisses leprechaun hunting
jokes.punchline: Charlie insists he's almost caught one despite years of failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie delusion. Works because of established character trait and his sincere conviction.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm, like, this close.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: I don't even know if I've set up my glue traps for him yet.
jokes.setup: Charlie's leprechaun hunting plans
jokes.punchline: He plans to catch a mythical creature with glue traps
jokes.explanation: The mundane approach to magical creature capture is absurd and perfectly Charlie. Economy of language sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't even know if I've set up my glue traps for him yet.

jokes.text: Flute conversation escalation ending with 'Everybody go get a flute!'
jokes.setup: Mac mentions wanting to get a flute
jokes.punchline: It immediately escalates to everyone abandoning the meeting for flutes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the gang latches onto random ideas and abandons responsibilities. Natural build.

jokes.text: Frank snoring during Dennis's presentation
jokes.setup: Dennis predicted Frank would fall asleep
jokes.punchline: Frank is literally snoring, proving Dennis right
jokes.explanation: Predictable but satisfying payoff to the earlier setup. Classic visual comedy beat.

jokes.text: FRANK (Irish accent): Top o' the morning, you filthy lads. I'm fixing to have a whale of a time in this here mobile pub.
jokes.setup: Dennis wants authentic, non-stereotypical experience
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately does offensive Irish stereotype voice
jokes.explanation: Frank immediately undermining Dennis's vision with exactly what he doesn't want. Good character conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Top o' the morning, you filthy lads. I'm fixing to have a whale of a time in this here mobile pub.

jokes.text: Frank's 'Crazy Paddy' Irish stereotype character
jokes.setup: Frank appearing in full Irish stereotype costume
jokes.punchline: His offensive caricature complete with exaggerated accent
jokes.explanation: Frank's commitment to inappropriate characters, though the stereotype itself isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Frank's 'Crazy Paddy' Irish stereotype character

jokes.text: FRANK: I brought my piece in case anybody gets out of line.
jokes.setup: Frank dressed as stereotype Irish character
jokes.punchline: He's armed and ready for violence at their mobile bar
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual mention of bringing a gun to their business venture. Dark escalation delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I brought my piece in case anybody gets out of line.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: 'Cause beer's not a food. When was the last time you ate a beer?
jokes.setup: Mac suggests food coloring for beer instead of paint
jokes.punchline: Charlie's logic that beer isn't food so food coloring won't work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic that's both completely wrong and internally consistent. Perfect absurdist reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Cause beer's not a food. When was the last time you ate a beer?

jokes.text: MAC: When was the last time you drank paint?
jokes.setup: Charlie's nonsense about beer not being food
jokes.punchline: Mac points out the obvious flaw - Charlie is drinking paint
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that highlights how insane Charlie's behavior is. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: When was the last time you drank paint?

jokes.text: Charlie's green tongue from drinking paint
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether Charlie has been drinking paint
jokes.punchline: Visual confirmation - his tongue is green from paint
jokes.explanation: Strong visual payoff confirming Charlie's paint consumption. Gross-out humor that works for the character.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: It's not bad.
jokes.setup: Mac's disgust at Charlie drinking paint
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual assessment that paint tastes 'not bad'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's nonchalant response to something horrifying. Perfect character beat showing his complete disconnect from normal responses.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's not bad.

jokes.text: CUSTOMER: Is this, like, a car service thing?
jokes.setup: Dennis's complicated mobile bar concept
jokes.punchline: Customer immediately asks if it's a car service, the simple idea Mac suggested
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Dennis's overcomplicated idea confuses customers who want the simple version.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Is this, like, a car service thing?

jokes.text: DENNIS: No, it's not a goddamn car service. Why does everyone...
jokes.setup: Second person asking about car service
jokes.punchline: Dennis's frustration reveals this keeps happening
jokes.explanation: Dennis's mounting frustration as his genius concept is repeatedly misunderstood. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, it's not a goddamn car service. Why does everyone...

jokes.text: DEE (Irish accent): You get in that wagon right now or I'm gonna sock you a good one right in the kisser!
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to avoid offensive stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately does aggressive Irish stereotype with threat of violence
jokes.explanation: Dee undermining Dennis just like Frank did. Threatening customers while doing bad accent adds to the awfulness.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You get in that wagon right now or I'm gonna sock you a good one right in the kisser!

jokes.text: CUSTOMER: Oh, wait, so is this, like, a bang bus?
jokes.setup: Customers trying to understand their business model
jokes.punchline: Customer thinks it might be a pornographic setup
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of customer confusion. Their sketchy operation is raising red flags about sexual exploitation.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Oh, wait, so is this, like, a bang bus?

jokes.text: Dennis's overcomplicated app/QR code ordering system
jokes.setup: Customer wants to buy beer with cash
jokes.punchline: Dennis insists on unnecessarily complex digital ordering system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - overcomplicating simple transactions with tech buzzwords and unnecessary steps.

jokes.text: DEE: This is like all foam. FRANK: Dee, you goddamn bitch!
jokes.setup: Dee trying to serve beer in moving vehicle
jokes.punchline: The beer is all foam and Frank immediately calls her a bitch
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of mobile bar not working combined with Frank's immediate verbal abuse. Typical gang dysfunction.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: This is like all foam.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Dee, you goddamn bitch!

jokes.text: Customers trying to call cops, gang confiscating phones
jokes.setup: Business idea going wrong
jokes.punchline: Gang resorts to confiscating phones to prevent police calls
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how their incompetence leads to actual criminality. Dark turn that feels earned.

dialogue.line: Customers trying to call cops, gang confiscating phones

jokes.text: FRANK: We just drove the snakes out of Philly, the way St. Patrick did.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking if they just kidnapped and robbed people
jokes.punchline: Frank frames it as honoring St. Patrick's tradition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback recontextualizing their crimes as cultural celebration. Perfect Frank logic and excellent structure.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: We just drove the snakes out of Philly, the way St. Patrick did.

jokes.text: DENNIS: So, what you're saying is, technically, we gave those guys a very authentic St. Paddy's Day experience.
jokes.setup: Frank's St. Patrick justification
jokes.punchline: Dennis buys into the logic, finding comfort in the rationalization
jokes.explanation: Dennis's need for intellectual justification leads him to embrace Frank's insane logic. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, what you're saying is, technically, we gave those guys a very authentic St. Paddy's Day experience.

jokes.text: MAC: Irish honor system... It's an ancient Irish tradition. Know your history, bro.
jokes.setup: Mac implementing self-serve bar system
jokes.punchline: He invents fake Irish tradition to justify laziness
jokes.explanation: Mac's confident fake history and condescending 'know your history' sells the absurdity. Good character trait.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Irish honor system... It's an ancient Irish tradition. Know your history, bro.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: I captured a leprechaun, okay? You wouldn't say, 'I kidnapped Bigfoot,' right? You'd say, 'I captured Bigfoot.'
jokes.setup: Mac says Charlie kidnapped a little person
jokes.punchline: Charlie's semantic argument about capturing mythical creatures vs kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - technically correct linguistic argument that completely misses the moral issue. Perfectly absurd reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I captured a leprechaun, okay? You wouldn't say, 'I kidnapped Bigfoot,' right? You'd say, 'I captured Bigfoot.'

jokes.text: CHARLIE: 'Cause the basement door was locked, so unless he's a being who's unrestrained by the laws of the physical world, he's not getting down here, man.
jokes.setup: Mac arguing the person is human, not a leprechaun
jokes.punchline: Charlie's logic that locked door proves supernatural nature
jokes.explanation: Charlie's ironclad detective logic ignoring obvious explanations. The formal language makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: 'Cause the basement door was locked, so unless he's a being who's unrestrained by the laws of the physical world, he's not getting down here, man.

jokes.text: MAC: Are you now or have you ever been a leprechaun?
jokes.setup: Mac deciding to ask direct questions
jokes.punchline: Formal interrogation language for asking about being a mythical creature
jokes.explanation: Treating leprechaun status like Cold War communist investigation. Good escalation of their serious approach to nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are you now or have you ever been a leprechaun?

jokes.text: VICTIM: No, I'm not a goddamn leprechaun! Please...
jokes.setup: Formal interrogation about leprechaun status
jokes.punchline: Desperate, obvious denial from clearly human victim
jokes.explanation: The obvious human desperation contrasts with their serious investigation. Uncomfortable but necessary for the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Victim
dialogue.line: No, I'm not a goddamn leprechaun! Please...

jokes.text: MAC: You made a wish, which the leprechaun clearly granted.
jokes.setup: Charlie casually mentions wishing for a hose
jokes.punchline: Mac interprets convenient hose appearance as magic wish fulfillment
jokes.explanation: Mac getting convinced by coincidence and applying magical thinking to mundane events. Good escalation of delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You made a wish, which the leprechaun clearly granted.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: I wish I could live forever? What are you doing?! MAC: I wish you don't.
jokes.setup: Charlie about to waste a wish
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately wastes his own wish out of spite
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Mac's horror at Charlie's wish leads to equally stupid counter-wish. Dark comedy of mutual destruction.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I wish I could live forever? What are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I wish you don't.

jokes.text: DENNIS: I can't tell if you're doing a thing now, or if this is just who you've become.
jokes.setup: Dee's confusing mix of accents and personas
jokes.punchline: Dennis's existential confusion about whether this is performance or personality
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on character degradation over time. Brilliant line that works on multiple levels - performance and existential.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I can't tell if you're doing a thing now, or if this is just who you've become.

jokes.text: DENNIS: Well, if you don't comply with me, then you're gonna end up on the weird one, okay?
jokes.setup: Dennis taking photos for his website
jokes.punchline: Implies he has a 'weird' website for non-compliant victims
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation suggesting Dennis has multiple disturbing websites. Perfectly in character predatory behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, if you don't comply with me, then you're gonna end up on the weird one, okay?

jokes.text: CHARLIE: Aw, what's the point, dude? There's no water pressure. I feel like I'm giving him a baby bath.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to torture someone with a hose
jokes.punchline: Disappointed the torture is ineffective, compares to gentle baby bath
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual disappointment in torture effectiveness with innocent comparison. Perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Aw, what's the point, dude? There's no water pressure. I feel like I'm giving him a baby bath.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: He probably killed it with his leprechaun magic, anyway.
jokes.setup: Mac points out Charlie killed the snake by suffocating it
jokes.punchline: Charlie blames leprechaun magic instead of accepting responsibility
jokes.explanation: Charlie's refusal to accept obvious causation, preferring magical explanation. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He probably killed it with his leprechaun magic, anyway.

jokes.text: VICTIM: Okay, fine, you got me. I'm a leprechaun... I live in a faraway land, and I was transported here by a giant magical rainbow.
jokes.setup: Victim being tortured to admit he's a leprechaun
jokes.punchline: Finally gives in with stereotypical leprechaun story to stop torture
jokes.explanation: Victim's desperate attempt to end torture by giving them what they want. Uncomfortable but necessary for plot.

dialogue.speaker: Victim
dialogue.line: Okay, fine, you got me. I'm a leprechaun... I live in a faraway land, and I was transported here by a giant magical rainbow.

jokes.text: VICTIM: And you never got me Lucky Charms. CHARLIE & MAC: (laughing) Oh! That's it! You caught a leprechaun!
jokes.setup: Victim adding details to fake leprechaun story
jokes.punchline: Lucky Charms reference convinces Charlie and Mac completely
jokes.explanation: Cereal mascot reference as 'proof' of leprechaun identity shows their complete gullibility when they want to believe.

dialogue.speaker: Victim
dialogue.line: And you never got me Lucky Charms.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie & Mac
dialogue.line: (laughing) Oh! That's it! You caught a leprechaun!

jokes.text: MAC: Dude, he's talking about that gay bar down on Locust Street-- the Rainbow.
jokes.setup: Leprechaun says gold is at bottom of rainbow
jokes.punchline: Mac realizes 'Rainbow' refers to local gay bar
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from mythical rainbow to real establishment. Sets up Mac's suspicious eagerness to go alone.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dude, he's talking about that gay bar down on Locust Street-- the Rainbow.

jokes.text: MAC: Right. I'm gonna go check it out, alone... you'll be here, alone.
jokes.setup: Mac volunteering to check the Rainbow bar
jokes.punchline: His suspicious emphasis on going alone reveals his real motivation
jokes.explanation: Mac's transparent excuse to visit gay bar alone. Good character work showing repressed desires.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Right. I'm gonna go check it out, alone... you'll be here, alone.

jokes.text: DENNIS: The business model's flawless. If you knew how to drive and you knew how to serve a goddamn beer, we wouldn't be in this mess.
jokes.setup: Their business completely failed
jokes.punchline: Dennis blames execution while calling model flawless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - his ideas can never be wrong, only poorly executed by others. Classic character trait.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The business model's flawless. If you knew how to drive and you knew how to serve a goddamn beer, we wouldn't be in this mess.

jokes.text: Frank abandoning Dee by driving away
jokes.setup: Frank asks Dee to check gas cap
jokes.punchline: He drives away immediately, abandoning her
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank cruelty delivered as practical joke. Good visual comedy with mean-spirited twist.

jokes.text: FRANK: That bitch was dragging us down. DENNIS: Yeah. She was driving me crazy, man.
jokes.setup: Frank abandons Dee
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately agrees and joins in badmouthing her
jokes.explanation: Dennis's quick betrayal of his sister shows his opportunistic loyalty. Fits character but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That bitch was dragging us down.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. She was driving me crazy, man.

jokes.text: DENNIS: Oh, shit. She didn't put the gas cap on. FRANK: Oh, is it open for real?
jokes.setup: Frank tricks Dee about fake gas cap problem
jokes.punchline: She actually forgot to replace the gas cap
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - Frank's made-up excuse turns out to be real. Karma for their meanness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, shit. She didn't put the gas cap on.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, is it open for real?

jokes.text: FRANK: It ain't a metaphor. I fell asleep, crashed into a wall, the engine burst into flames, and I had to drive it into a river.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks why Frank speaks in metaphors
jokes.punchline: Frank literally did all the disaster metaphors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing Frank literally destroyed their business in the most dramatic way possible. Perfect pacing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It ain't a metaphor. I fell asleep, crashed into a wall, the engine burst into flames, and I had to drive it into a river.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: Well, you may be a man. You may be a leprechaun. But only one thing's for sure: you're in the wrong basement.
jokes.setup: Charlie about to torture the victim
jokes.punchline: Ominous threat delivered like movie villain monologue
jokes.explanation: Charlie channeling movie villain with theatrical menace. Dark comedy of his serious approach to insane situation.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, you may be a man. You may be a leprechaun. But only one thing's for sure: you're in the wrong basement.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: (chuckles) No, I've tied a leprechaun to a chair, and, uh, yeah, I was gonna cut him to pieces.
jokes.setup: Gang discovers Charlie about to torture someone
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual, cheerful description of planned dismemberment
jokes.explanation: Charlie's cheerful matter-of-fact delivery of horrific plan. The casual tone makes it darkly funny rather than disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: (chuckles) No, I've tied a leprechaun to a chair, and, uh, yeah, I was gonna cut him to pieces.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: like, 'I'm a pickpocket. I'm a pickpocket. Stop hurting me. I'm not a leprechaun.' And I just thought that was, like, a metaphor for, you know, 'I-I am a leprechaun.'
jokes.setup: Mac reveals victim is actually a pickpocket
jokes.punchline: Charlie interpreted literal confessions as metaphorical leprechaun admission
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie logic - interpreting desperate literal pleas as coded magical confession. Perfect character moment showing his complete detachment from reality.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: like, 'I'm a pickpocket. I'm a pickpocket. Stop hurting me. I'm not a leprechaun.' And I just thought that was, like, a metaphor for, you know, 'I-I am a leprechaun.'

jokes.text: CHARLIE: Oh, the luck of the Irish.
jokes.setup: Gang decides to keep stolen wallets
jokes.punchline: Charlie frames their criminal success as Irish luck
jokes.explanation: Good callback tying their crimes to holiday theme. Charlie's innocent delivery of the line sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, the luck of the Irish.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: Oh, shit, that leprechaun just flew up a rainbow!
jokes.setup: They throw victim out of truck
jokes.punchline: Charlie's paint-addled perception sees magical leprechaun escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to Charlie's delusion - paint hallucinations make him see magical escape. Good payoff to paint drinking setup.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, shit, that leprechaun just flew up a rainbow!

jokes.text: MAC: Stop drinking paint, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Charlie's obvious hallucination
jokes.punchline: Mac's weary, practical response to supernatural claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on the episode - Mac's exhausted straight-man response to Charlie's paint-induced fantasy. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Stop drinking paint, Charlie.

tmdb_overview: Dennis tries to create an innovative mobile Paddy's pub experience but runs into trouble when customers refuse to cooperate, while Charlie and Mac attempt to catch a thieving leprechaun.
